contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 5
- Description: @⁨Liz Wong - Shen Zhen Fireworks Dealer⁩ now that the prototype that @⁨Kaon⁩ developed is up and running and is a visible cost center it makes sense to further clarify on the following the profit center side of things to figure out the model’s sustainability as it scales otherwise Aora will be forced down the VC route. At which point my sense is they will just boot the DAO out of the equation along with my involvement 🤣
  
  A few questions to think through 
  
  1. How do you envision Aora to be over the next 18 months?
  
  2. ⁠are you envisioning teachers or students to be using what @⁨Kaon⁩ built?
  
  3. ⁠are you envisioning them to be using it for free or a fee?
  
  4. ⁠if a fee, how much to charge? 
  
  5. ⁠if free, then how are you planning to cover the cost of maintaining the server cost he raised? 
  
  6. ⁠the server cost incurred for running Sophia and Edgar is covered by another ecosystem partner. Ownership of Sophia and Edgar are apportioned based on the TDG token holdings based on tokenomics of the DAO based on 100TDG per hour and 1TDG for USD$1 contributed 
  
  7. ⁠I am not sure if you really want that ownership of @⁨Kaon⁩ asset as well as Aora’s assets to fall under the DAO. 
  
  8. ⁠if operating cost of @⁨Kaon⁩ asset is going to be covered under Aora through sales of merchandise then it is probably a good time to figure out what the go to market execution roadmap is as well as what the expected user base and revenue volume is at each stage of the user base growth if you are planning to charge for the usage of the platform. However if just planning to sell physical merchandise as the primary monetization strategy then part of the margins will need to be retain to cover ongoing operating cost of the servers that @⁨Kaon⁩ surfaced 
  
  9. ⁠just to clarify Agroverse is not a company. It is a brand and a supply chain that is enabled by a network of companies
  
  10. ⁠as I detailed in the execution roadmap I shared yesterday, Santos will know how many chocolate molds he will need once he knows the expected volume from Aora needs delivered on a yearly basis. To facilitate the discussion I have indicate as a placeholder of 81% chocolate but Fatima brought up that folks might prefer a different concentration of sugar versus cacao. So we need market visibility into China for that
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 8.33
- Attached Filename: IMG_2491.png
